content:
🔹 What Works Well:
• Alternating A & B Weeks ensures a mix of discussion-led & self-guided learning.
• Pet Peeve Reflections give students a low-stakes way to engage every week.
• Freedom in text selection for final papers encourages self-driven research.

🔹 Possible Refinements:
1. More Self-Guided Research Checkpoints 📌
• Have students submit research progress updates every two weeks in Weeks 11-15.
• These can be informal, completion-based check-ins (not graded).
2. More Student-Led Discussions 🎤
• Instead of instructor-led every A Week, assign student discussion leaders who prepare one key question to facilitate discussion.
3. Optional One-on-One Meetings for Final Projects ☕
• Offer 15-minute office hours slots in Weeks 12-14 for students who want targeted feedback.

✅ Why This Works:
• Gives students more control over their final project without sacrificing accountability.
